paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then Joseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood before him; and he cried, Cause every man to go out from me. And there stood no man with him, while Joseph made himself known unto his brethren.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he wept aloud: and the Egyptians heard, and the house of Pharaoh heard.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Joseph said unto his brethren, I am Joseph; doth my father yet live? And his brethren could not answer him; for they were troubled at his presence.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Joseph said unto his brethren, Come near to me, I pray you. And they came near. And he said, I am Joseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And now be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither: for God did send me before you to preserve life.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For these two years hath the famine been in the land: and there are yet five years, in which there shall be neither plowing nor harvest.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God sent me before you to preserve you a remnant in the earth, and to save you alive by a great deliverance.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So now it was not you that sent me hither, but God: and he hath made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Haste ye, and go up to my father, and say unto him, Thus saith thy son Joseph, God hath made me lord of all Egypt: come down unto me, tarry not;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and thou shalt dwell in the land of Goshen, and thou shalt be near unto me, thou, and thy children, and thy children`s children, and thy flocks, and thy herds, and all that thou hast:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and there will I nourish thee; for there are yet five years of famine; lest thou come to poverty, thou, and thy household, and all that thou hast.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And, behold, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that it is my mouth that speaketh unto you.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And ye shall tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that ye have seen: and ye shall haste and bring down my father hither.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he fell upon his brother Benjamin`s neck, and wept; and Benjamin wept upon his neck.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he kissed all his brethren, and wept upon them: and after that his brethren talked with him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the report thereof was heard in Pharaoh`s house, saying, Joseph`s brethren are come: and it pleased Pharaoh well, and his servants.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, Say unto thy brethren, This do ye: lade your beasts, and go, get you unto the land of Canaan;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and take your father and your households, and come unto me: and I will give you the good of the land of Egypt, and ye shall eat the fat of the land.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now thou art commanded, this do ye: take you wagons out of the land of Egypt for your little ones, and for your wives, and bring your father, and come.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Also regard not your stuff; for the good of all the land of Egypt is yours.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the sons of Israel did so: and Joseph gave them wagons, according to the commandment of Pharaoh, and gave them provision for the way.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: To all of them he gave each man changes of raiment; but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver, and five changes of raiment.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And to his father he sent after this manner: ten asses laden with the good things of Egypt, and ten she-asses laden with grain and bread and provision for his father by the way.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So he sent his brethren away, and they departed: and he said unto them, See that ye fall not out by the way.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they went up out of Egypt, and came into the land of Canaan unto Jacob their father.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they told him, saying, Joseph is yet alive, and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt. And his heart fainted, for he believed them not.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they told him all the words of Joseph, which he had said unto them: and when he saw the wagons which Joseph had sent to carry him, the spirit of Jacob their father revived:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and Israel said, It is enough; Joseph my son is yet alive: I will go and see him before I die.
